sources: Benzinga (news/WIIM) + Finnhub (analyst consensus) + SEC EDGAR (filings) + live prices + Jaleel Capital framework

macro.headline: Hawkish Fed, energy & geopolitics in the driver's seat
macro.body: Fed Chair Warsh scrapped forward guidance and the dot plot leans toward hikes, not cuts, so long-duration high-multiple growth (NOW, ADBE, NVDA, IOT) stays rate-sensitive. Energy and the Strait of Hormuz headlines are the live swing factor on inflation.

risk.detail: FY guide cut (Jun 4): Americas comps −5% (5th straight), tariffs gutted margins, China stalled.
FY26 growth cut 10.5→7–8.5%; Watchman/EP/urology soft; mgmt guides flat through Q3.
Beat & raised (Jun 11) yet sold off; AI-moat fear (Claude Design), CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis — the narrative, not the numbers, drives it.
China export-license tightening + Senate scrutiny; the memory/chip complex swings on AI-spending sentiment.
No bad news — strong Q1, IBM AI deal. But it's the biggest weight + a high multiple; in a hawkish-Fed software de-rate it leads the drawdown.
Trump MFN pricing deals (Gilead, Novartis signed) + import tariffs put a pricing-pressure overhang on the whole pharma cluster.
FY EPS guide cut on deal financing + a weak flu season; revenue raised. Defensive — a yellow flag, not a red one.

sectors.driver: AI-related stocks face renewed pressure following Samsung Electronics' p…
sectors.note: As AI-related stocks face renewed pressure following Samsung Electronics' preliminary second-quarter results, with investors focusing on concerns over AI infrastructure spending and demand. Reports that China's DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip have also raised concerns about future demand for third-party AI semiconductors from companies such as Nvidia and Huawei.

sectors.driver: The House and Senate sent a bipartisan housing bill designed to lower ho…
sectors.note: After the House and Senate sent a bipartisan housing bill designed to lower housing costs for President Trump's signature. Sentiment was further boosted by KB Home’s better-than-expected second-quarter sales results.

Shares of data center infrastructure-related companies are trading lower. Catalysts including the the sector outlook following Samsung's preliminary Q2 results and reports suggesting DeepSeek will develop its own AI inference chips are weighing on the tech hardware industry.

Shares of foundry, fabricator and integrated device manufacturing semiconductor companies are trading lower as AI-related stocks face renewed pressure following Samsung Electronics' preliminary second-quarter results, with investors focusing on concerns over AI infrastructure spending and demand. Reports that China's DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip have also raised concerns about future demand for third-party AI semiconductors from companies such as Nvidia and Huawei.
